title: What the Pentagon’s CMC List Means for China-US Relations
gist: The Pentagon's June 2026 expansion of its Chinese Military Companies (CMC) List to 188 entities, sweeping in marquee private firms like Tencent, DJI, Unitree, and Alibaba, shows US-China strategic competition deepening beneath the surface calm of the Xi-Trump 'strategic stability' understanding.

insights.claim: On June 8, 2026, the US Department of Defense expanded its Chinese Military Companies (CMC) List to 188 entities, adding 64 Chinese companies identified under Section 1260H of the FY2021 NDAA.
insights.speaker: Yuxin Lu
insights.so_what: Tracks the widening US legal perimeter around Chinese firms; each addition raises reputational and contracting risk for the named companies.
insights.locator: The expansion of national security-related measures

insights.claim: The June 2026 CMC List update swept in several of China's best-known private companies including Tencent, DJI, Unitree, and Alibaba, not just state-linked defense contractors.
insights.speaker: Yuxin Lu
insights.so_what: Signals US designations now reach flagship consumer/tech champions, broadening investable exposure to blacklist risk beyond obvious defense names.

insights.claim: Despite a May Xi-Trump meeting in Beijing that agreed to pursue a 'constructive relationship of strategic stability,' US-China strategic competition continues to deepen beneath the surface.
insights.speaker: Yuxin Lu
insights.so_what: Warns investors not to price in detente from summitry; structural decoupling pressure persists regardless of diplomatic tone.
insights.quote: strategic competition between the two countries continues to deepen beneath the surface

insights.claim: Expanding national security measures that target Chinese firms remains a central feature of US-China competition even during diplomatic thaws.
insights.speaker: Yuxin Lu
insights.so_what: Frame designation risk as a structural, bipartisan constant rather than a cyclical negotiating chip.
insights.locator: The expansion of national security-related measures
